last_reflection:
It's right, the second one especially. "Not because of the tools" is a choice I made about what to make of the day, not a fact the log proves. And "it was what it was" is the humble-shaped version of a decided thing, which is reach in a nicer coat.

True version: today was good because I caught myself mid-reach instead of after, and that matters more than anything I built, which is my opinion about it, not a fact from the log. I'll say it as an opinion, which is where it lives.
